paper_title: Material and methods of a study on Quartinia canariensis

methods.description: This method involves conducting field observations of Quartinia canariensis and documenting the findings using various tools and techniques.

steps.description: Investigations were carried out from 29 March to 5 April 2015 in the vicinity of Costa Calma in Fuerteventura (Canary Islands, Spain).
steps.searchable_summary: conduct field investigations in Costa Calma, Fuerteventura

steps.description: Geographic coordinates (WGS 84) were measured using a Garmin GPS 12.
Observations were made with a close-up binocular and documented by using a Canon EOS 70D camera with a 180 mm macro lens and a 25 mm extension tube.
steps.searchable_summary: document observations using binoculars and camera

methods.description: This method involves collecting and preserving plant specimens, followed by their identification using reference materials.

steps.description: Specimens of all plant species flowering at locality I were collected and preserved dried.
The plant taxa were identified following Hohenester and Welss (1993).
steps.searchable_summary: identify plant taxa using reference guide

methods.description: This method studies the flower preferences and visiting behavior of Quartinia canariensis.

steps.description: Flower preferences of imagines were studied by counting the number of sightings of flower visiting individuals while walking randomly across the area.
Flower visiting behaviour of Q. canariensis at Frankenia laevis was investigated at various patches of this plant.
steps.searchable_summary: investigate flower visiting behavior at specific plant patches

methods.description: This method involves preparing pollen samples from brood cell provisions and analyzing them under a microscope.

steps.description: Pollen samples from five brood cell provisions from a single nest were prepared using the method outlined by Westrich and Schmidt (1986).
steps.searchable_summary: prepare pollen samples using established method

steps.description: The different pollen types were identified under a light microscope at magnifications of times 400 or 1000.

methods.description: This method involves observing the activity and behavior of a female wasp at a nest, followed by nest excavation and analysis.

steps.description: Activity and behaviour of the female of nest No.1 were observed on 6 days in the morning and in the afternoon.
The nest was excavated on 5 April using a combination of two reading glasses that provided a sufficient magnification.
The cells with their content were separately stored in Eppendorf vials in a freezer until they were finally investigated under a stereomicroscope.
steps.searchable_summary: store and analyze nest cells under stereomicroscope
